category: multi-variant page where the default variant is a small sampler

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text. The product title, roaster, location, origin, region, process, variety, producer, altitude, roast level, harvest, tasting notes, sensory/producer text, variant price/size/currency, and in-stock availability are explicitly stated. Boolean inferences are also supported: not espresso ('Recommended for: Filter'), not decaf (no decaf indication and standard washed Ethiopia description), not blend (single-origin Ethiopia with specific region/producer), not coferment/infused (standard washed process described), specialty coffee supported by detailed origin/process/variety/altitude/harvest and specialty-style presentation.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is exactly what you’d expect: site header/navigation, breadcrumb, product title, price, variant selectors, add-to-cart, tasting notes, roast/use guidance, origin details, descriptive copy, a sampler note, roaster location, then policy/footer items. The voice is restrained and commercially natural for specialty coffee—specific but not overblown. Origin/process details are coherent and internally consistent: Ethiopia / Bensa / Sidama / Hamasho washing station, washed process, Ethiopian JARC varieties 74158 and 74110, altitude 2,200–2,350 masl, and a current harvest window all make sense together. The tasting notes and brew recommendation fit a light washed Sidama profile well. The descriptive paragraphs read like real roaster copy, with believable process language and sensory framing. The variant data block also looks realistic: clear size/title/name mapping, plausible SKUs, sensible gram weights, and pricing that scales credibly from sampler to 250g to 1kg. Inventory counts are believable. The only very minor tell is that the embedded variant block is a bit cleaner and more manually readable than many raw html-to-text outputs, but it is still entirely plausible as extracted structured product metadata.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: Apricot, jasmine, lemon candy, black tea
This lot comes to us from the high-elevation community of Hamasho in Bensa, Sidama. We are roasting it light to keep the florals up front and the cup clean, with a sweet lemon-candy acidity and an apricot-like finish as it cools.
At the Hamasho washing station, smallholder growers deliver ripe cherries grown around 2,200 masl and above. The coffee is depulped, fermented in water, washed, and dried slowly on raised beds. The result is a delicate washed Ethiopia with jasmine aromatics, black-tea structure, and a sparkling citrus sweetness.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Apricot, jasmine, lemon candy, black tea

The result is a delicate washed Ethiopia with jasmine aromatics, black-tea structure, and a sparkling citrus sweetness.
